$comment: c88 (D-RUNTIME-MAIN-ARGC-ARGV): the program-entry argc/argv RUN witness. The shipped elf64-*-linux-exec formats declare `processArgs` = stack-vector (SysV AMD64 psABI 3.4.1 / AAPCS64 Linux: argc at [sp+0], the in-place NULL-terminated argv vector at sp+8), so the entry trampoline loads argc into argGprs[0] and LEAs the vector's address into argGprs[1] BEFORE calling main. The runner spawns with no extra args -> main must see argc==1 + a non-empty argv[0] (the program path) + argv[1]==NULL -> exit 42; each failed layer exits a distinct 90..94 (see main.c). The pe64 leg is COMPILE-ONLY (runOn empty): the Windows OS entry receives NO C argument vector - the msvcrt out-parameter route (__getmainargs) is the deferred D-RUNTIME-PE-MAIN-ARGS arm, so a pe run would read entry-register garbage argc (the pre-c88 behavior, harmless for the whole main(void) corpus, wrong for THIS main). The 2-arg + argv-CONTENT witnesses live in the c88 CLI matrix (the ctest runner passes no program argv). RED-on-disable: remove `processArgs` from either shipped ELF exec JSON (runtime-read config) -> that leg exits 90 (garbage argc) instead of 42.
